headline: Orang Asli school dropout rate reaches 42% nationally
context: Education Ministry data showed a 42% dropout rate among Orang Asli students before completing secondary school in 2024.

cards.big: 42% of Orang Asli students drop out before completing Form 5
cards.sub: National average dropout rate is only 1.2% by comparison

cards.big: Education Act 1996 makes primary education compulsory for all children
cards.sub: Remote locations and cultural insensitivity in curriculum cited as barriers

cards.big: CRC Article 28 requires states to make education accessible to indigenous children
cards.sub: Orang Asli communities have demanded mother-tongue instruction since 2015

cards.big: Average distance to secondary school for Orang Asli students is 28 kilometres
cards.sub: Hostel conditions reported as poor by 67% of parents in JAKOA survey

cards.big: 42% dropout is not failure of students but failure of the education system
Education Ministry allocated RM12 million for Orang Asli schools in Budget 2025
